title: Events of the Second Impact
wikitext:
[]: Events of the "Second Impact"             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Nov 05, 2011 6:40 am So, the "Cities after EoE" thread got me thinking, and I realized I don't know the events of the 2nd Impact as much as I'd like. So, I understand the premise that the show runs with, what with Adam sending off the angels in all directions and releasing an Anti AT field (right?). But, I don't know the science of it. What caused the Antarctic to melt? What caused all the devastation? Is it just that the melted Antarctic caused massive scale flooding and ocean rising, which caused, what, species extinction and consequently food chain disruption? So, from a science perspective, not an Eva perspective, what happened during the 2nd impact?

[]: Re: Events of the "First Impact"             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Nov 05, 2011 10:11 am CJD wrote: So, the "Cities after EoE" thread got me thinking, and I realized I don't know the events of the 1st Impact as much as I'd like. So, I understand the premise that the show runs with, what with Adam sending off the angels in all directions and releasing an Anti AT field (right?). But, I don't know the science of it. What caused the Antarctic to melt? What caused all the devastation? Is it just that the melted Antarctic caused massive scale flooding and ocean rising, which caused, what, species extinction and consequently food chain disruption?

[]: Re: Events of the "First Impact"             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Nov 05, 2011 12:24 pm symbv wrote: Think the above events were caused by the Second Impact ?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Nov 05, 2011 1:13 pm Basically it's never made clear because if it was it wouldn't make much sense. Possibly the big explosion that immediately follows Katsuragi stowing Misato in the escape pod (or whatever the hell it is) just exerted enough force to liquefy everything? Or possibly it was when Adam itself blew up shortly afterwards - something that as far as I know is never in any way shown on screen (but logically must have happened, otherwise everyone on Earth would have gotten anti-AT-fielded and all that)? Or a combination of the two? At any rate, it seems pretty clear that the vast majority of Antarctic ice cap just melted, causing big tsunamis everywhere, general panic, etcetera. Raising sea levels by some 50-60 meters, too (which I feel like would drown Tokyo and in fact most Japanese major cities entirely, although I'm not an expert on Japanese geography) seems to have been part of it - although coastlines as shown in ep06 are minimally altered, so make of that what you will. It definitely is a pretty confusing event, and we never really get proper coverage of it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 05, 2011 4:03 pm It's quite a bit more than just melting all the ice... the continent of Antarctica (yes, there's land under there) was also blown to pieces, probably in some sort of attempt to raise the White Moon before everything got all screwed up. The combined effect of all this explosion stuff is that the Earth's Axis was thrown off (whether that means the crust slipped or the whole planet was given a wobble is an open question), which is why the seasons are mentioned to be all crazy. Japan is on the equator now, while the US shifted up towards the arctic circle, pretty much where Canada is now.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Nov 05, 2011 4:58 pm Japan has perpetual summer. What is the weather in Europe and in North America?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 05, 2011 6:55 pm The new north pole would be somewhere around the tip of Greenland, so a much larger portion of NA is in the arctic circle now, while Alaska will actually warm up a little. Europe is going to be rotated such that England, France, Spain and so on will be the new Scandinavia and the Balkans will be nearly in the tropics, but most of the eurozone will be in roughly the same climate band. This is making a minimal change though, just a straight N/S shift to put Japan in the northern tropics. If it's closer to the equator or even the southern tropics now the new North Pole could be in the middle of the Atlantic, and that's without considering an E/W shift as well. It's entirely possible to make Paris the new Pole if you open the box to that level of change. What any of this means for weather patterns though is a question I can't answer.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Nov 05, 2011 6:58 pm NemZ wrote: The new north pole would be somewhere around the tip of Greenland, so a much larger portion of NA is in the arctic circle now, while Alaska will actually warm up a little. Europe is going to be rotated such that England, France, Spain and so on will be the new Scandinavia and the Balkans will be nearly in the tropics, but most of the eurozone will be in roughly the same climate band. This is making a minimal change though, just a straight N/S shift to put Japan in the northern tropics. If it's closer to the equator or even the southern tropics now the new North Pole could be in the middle of the Atlantic, and that's without considering an E/W shift as well. It's entirely possible to make Paris the new Pole if you open the box to that level of change. What any of this means for weather patterns though is a question I can't answer.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Nov 06, 2011 12:39 am So I put this together and... yeah, Europe and the US are way more screwed then I first guessed. SA is looking mighty happy... or they would be if they hadn't been demolished by 2I.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Nov 06, 2011 9:23 am NemZ wrote: So I put this together and... yeah, Europe and the US are way more screwed then I first guessed.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sun Nov 06, 2011 1:23 pm NemZ wrote: So I put this together and... yeah, Europe and the US are way more screwed then I first guessed. SA is looking mighty happy... or they would be if they hadn't been demolished by 2I.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Nov 06, 2011 4:53 pm Fireand'chutes77 wrote: So it's certainly possible that 2I wonked the Earth's axis, but in a minor, "factoid" kind of way that people quote to get their heads around just how Holy F*king Mother Of God huge 2I was.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Nov 06, 2011 6:38 pm Fireand'chutes77 wrote: It's my impression that Japan's new season's aren't because, OMG, Tokyo's now at 0 latitude. (That's a 35-degree shift!) It's because of jet-streams and ocean currents got altered by all the ice melting.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Nov 06, 2011 8:37 pm Bagheera wrote: Of course, with the loss of that ice cap Japan should be under water, but whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Nov 06, 2011 8:48 pm symbv wrote: What I read is that if all the ice cap melted, the sea level would rise by between 60 to 75 meters. I guess you mean the plains of Japan should be under water not the whole island?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Nov 06, 2011 9:27 pm So when you said Japan you meant Japan society/infrastructure/population, fair enough then...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Nov 06, 2011 10:22 pm Maybe a bit of several causes, but I do like the idea that 2I was a big enough event to send the earth's crust out of whack. Has more "HOLY SHIT" factor going for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sun Nov 06, 2011 10:32 pm symbv wrote: What I read is that if all the ice cap melted, the sea level would rise by between 60 to 75 meters. I guess you mean the plains of Japan should be under water not the whole island?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Nov 06, 2011 10:52 pm Yeah, the one he links at the bottom of the page is actually a lot better for looking at which cities get drowned and which don't, but is somewhat limited in range. And I'm inclined to say that Anno really didn't think too hard about the effects of Antarctica melting, because, well, look at this shit. [url]http://flood.firetree.net/?ll=48.3416,14.6777&z=13&m=60[/url] Sailed from Wilhelmshaven my ass. Maybe in a submarine.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Nov 06, 2011 10:58 pm Fireand'chutes77 wrote: I found some interesting applets that let you submerge the world's coastlines. There are probably more detailed ones out there, but this is a rough guide. http://merkel.zoneo.net/Topo/Applet/

So, I understand the premise that the show runs with, what with Adam sending off the angels in all directions and releasing an Anti AT field (right?). But, I don't know the science of
